original: NASA publicly unveils the space shuttle Enterprise in Palmdale, California, named after the Star Trek Enterprise, with the cast attending
Harriet Tubman1st escapes slavery in Maryland with two of her brothers
Kevin Clash, American puppeteer (Sesame Street, 1984-2012 "Elmo"), born in Baltimore, Maryland
Charles Martinet, American actor (voice of Mario in Nintendo video games 1996-2023), born in San Jose, California
Dutch scientistAntonie van Leeuwenhoekis the first to report the existence of bacteria
Leon Foucault, French physicist whose pendulum proved that the Earth rotates, born in Paris (d. 1868)
The first version of the Linux kernel (0.01) is released to the Internet
Konstantin Tsiolkovsky, Russian pioneer in rocket and space research, born in Izhevskoye, Ryazan Governorate, Russian Empire (d. 1935)
British rock band "The Who" make US television debut, performing "My Generation" on "The Smothers Brothers Comedy Hour", and detonatingKeith Moon's drum kit
Joshua Abraham Norton, an English-born resident of San Francisco, proclaims himself his Imperial MajestyEmperor NortonI, Emperor of the United States of America
World War I flying ace,the Red Baronof the German Luftstreitkräfte, wins his first aerial combat near Cambrai, France
Charles Darwins lands on Chatham in Galapagos archipelago
William Herscheldiscovers Mimas, satellite of Saturn

Oswald Garrison Villard Jr., American Engineer (electronics) who invented the over-the-horizon radar that could detect objects thousands of miles away and peer around the Earth's curvature, born in Dobbs Ferry, New York (d. 2004)
Zond 5 completes circumnavigation of Moon
Edgar Mitchell, NASA astronaut (Apollo 14), born in Hereford Texas (d. 2016)
Justice Department files 1st suit to end segregation in public schools
Polish workers under the leadership ofLech Wałęsafound the Solidarity movement at the Gdańsk Shipyard
US Constitution is signed by delegates at the Philadelphia Convention
US space officials announce selection of 9 new astronauts
John Goodricke, English deaf astronomer (first to notice that some variable stars were periodic), born in Groningen, Netherlands (d. 1786)
Scott Crossfield takes X-15 up for 1st powered flight
Minnesota Vikings' 1st NFL game (beat Chicago Bears 37-13)

The Beatles are paid a then-record $150,000 by baseball team owner Charles Finley for a concert at Municipal Stadium in Kansas City, Missouri, on a scheduled day off; the group adds the song "Kansas City"/"Hey, Hey, Hey, Hey" to their standard setlist, much to the delight of the crowd
Ernie Banksbecomes Chicago Cubs 1st black player
PopeJohn Paul IIarrives in San Francisco, meets with AIDS patients, and embraces an AIDS infected child
Denis Peyrony and Louis Capitan discover the skull of an adult male Neanderthal (La Ferrassie 1) during excavations in a rock shelter near La Ferrassie, France
Phillip W. Pratt patents his sprinkler system for extinguishing fires
Giuseppe Caspar Mezzofanti, Italian cardinal, linguist and hyperpolyglot (understood 70 languages), born in Bologna, Italy (d. 1849)
Michiyo Tsujimura, Japanese agricultural scientist and biochemist (components of green tea), born in Saitama Prefecture, Japan (d. 1969)
Lawrence Anthony, South Africa, conservationist and author, born in Johannesburg (d. 2012)
Reinhold Messner, Italian mountain climber (Mount Everest), born in Brixen (Bressanone), Italy
John Ritter, American actor and comedian (Three's Company, It, 8 Simple Rules), born in Burbank, California (d. 2003)
Baz Luhrmann, Australian film director (Romeo + Juliet, Moulin Rouge!), born in Sydney, Australia
Ken Kesey, American author (One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest; Sometimes a Great Notion), born in La Junta Colorado (d. 2001)
First class for escaped slaves taught by Mary Peake at Fortress Monroe Virginia (now Hampton University).
Rollout of 1st space shuttle orbiter Enterprise (OV-101)
Soyuz T-14 carries 3 cosmonauts to Salyut 7 space station
Thomas P. Stafford, American US Air Force officer and NASA astronaut (Gemini 6 and 9; Apollo 10 and 18), born in Weatherford, Oklahoma (d. 2024)[1]
Pamela Ann Melroy, American Major USAF and astronaut (STS 92), born in Palo Alto, California
Samuel T. Durrance, American scientist and astronaut (STS 35, STS 67), born in Tallahassee, Florida

Fran Tarkentonplays his first NFL Game against the Chicago Bears, coming off the bench to lead the Vikings to a 37-13 victory, also becoming the only QB to throw four touchdown passes in his first career game
In a quick re-match at Yankee Stadium, NYC,Rocky MarcianoKOsEzzard Charlesin the 8th round to retain his world heavyweight boxing title
Cleveland pitchers set AL record striking out 19 batters in 1st 9 innings
Yanks clinch pennant #22 on Mantle's 50th homer of year
Stephen Hales, English physiologist, chemist and inventor (first person to measure blood pressure), born in Bekesbourne, Kent, England (d. 1761)
Black students enter Clay elementary school in Kentucky
Russian Bolshoi Ballet dancers Leonid & Valentina Kozlov granted political asylum in US
Hurricane Hugo begins 4 day sweep through Caribbean, killing 62
Dutch ship Sardam arrives to rescue those shipwrecked on the Batavia, off coast of western Australia, discovering a group led by Jeronimus Cornelisz had murdered 124 of the survivors
San Francisco 49ers (formerly AAFC) play 1st NFL game, lose 21-17
San Francisco GiantsGaylord Perryno-hits St Louis Cardinals, 1-0
MLB Los Angeles Dodgers pitcherFernando Valenzuelasets NL rookie record with 8th shutout of season
Motown Records releasesThe Supremes' single "Baby Love"; written and produced by the Holland-Dozier-Holland team, it becomes their second consecutive #1 record
"Bad to the Bone" single byGeorge Thorogoodand the Destroyers first released

TV comedy series "M*A*S*H", adapted from the movie, starringAlan Alda,Loretta Swit, Wayne Rogers, and McLean Stevenson, debuts on CBS in the US and runs for 11 years, garnering 14 Emmy Awards and 1 Peabody
"Mission Impossible" premieres on CBS-TV
Frank Zappaconducts The Ensemble Modern in portions of his classical work "The Yellow Shark" in Frankfurt, Germany; receives a 20 minute standing ovation in what is his last public appearance
National Museum of Anthropology, the largest museum in Latin America, inaugurated by President Adolfo López Mateos in Mexico City
RCA Victor releases 1st 33 1/3 rpm recording -Beethoven's Fifth Symphony performed by the Philadelphia Orchestra, led by Leopold Stokowski at the Philadelphia Academy of Music
